headline: Oklo’s Losses Deepen. Why the Nuclear Start-Up Still Has Wall Street’s Attention.
summary: Oklo’s  losses deepened in its latest quarter, but the nuclear start-up remains committed to building a first-of-its-kind powerhouse that just cleared a major regulatory hurdle.  Oklo finished the quarter with $1.59 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $614.5 million in marketable debt securities.  With no revenue to analyze, Oklo’s health is difficult to gauge.
